changes.reason: Charm-wall breakaway is the highest-convexity small-sample VALID_0DTE profile: 5 trades, $985.6 PnL, 234.36 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, median TP 12.0. Keep admission geometry unchanged and test whether the current 12-point cap is overpaying hold time after prior wall-geometry gains.

Reduce only the charm-wall payout multiplier from the current 1.25 to the neutral grid value. This is a profile-local realization test, not a broad TP compression; it should reveal whether the profile's convex edge needs the extra target expansion or whether faster realization improves risk-adjusted efficiency.

Set the profile VWAP extension cap to the existing grid value 22, a modest trim from 25. The worst charm-wall MAE row had ES-VWAP 13.23, so this should preserve observed winners while preventing future wall-chase entries at excessive extension.

expected_effect: Should reduce charm_wall_breakaway_long exposure time and admission tail risk while preserving most of its convex PnL. Reject if the profile loses more than one trade without offsetting PnL/exposure improvement, total net profit drops below 98% of run_029, or the 12-point target compression cuts the profile's large winners enough to lower portfolio score.
label: charm-wall-realization-trim
rationale: Use one legal family, batch_d_charm_wall_breakaway_long. The current leader plateau has no slow >=12h trades and strong profit, so the next test should avoid broad entry deletion and avoid repeating failed squeeze/1DTE/no-0DTE TP compression. Charm-wall breakaway is small but high-value, with long average hold near 50 minutes and median TP 12.0; a local realization/extension trim is a controlled risk-adjusted experiment that does not touch protected pullback, charm-pin, squeeze, or fallback flow.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: charm_wall_breakaway_long contributes $985.6 from only 5 VALID_0DTE trades, with $197.1 average PnL, 234.36 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, no slow >=12h trades, but 50.5 minute average hold and -346.8 worst MAE. Prior admission geometry improved score, so this test keeps admission mostly intact and tunes realization only.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Charm Wall Realization Efficiency Trim
strategy_hypotheses.risk: The profile may require the 12-point expanded target to monetize rare convex wall-breakaway moves; compressing target geometry can reduce total PnL more than it improves exposure efficiency.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm-wall breakaway with require-breakout true, bounded distance above charm wall, and ES support already admitted by current geometry.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Profile PnL/exposure remains >=225, total net profit remains >=98% of run_029, completed trades remain within 10%, global worst MAE does not worsen beyond -513.4, and slow_12h remains 0.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: The analyzer repeatedly identifies charm-pin MAE as path-dependent: the worst charm-pin trade reached first meaningful profit only after about 80 minutes, while protected extended-VWAP winners resolve quickly. Static global filters overlap protected winners, so this remains a code-level edge rather than a safe config-only change.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Can overfit a small set of delayed recoveries or block profitable high-extension charm-pin winners unless validated against protected fast and extended-VWAP winner retention.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long high-extension or recent-high-rejection entries that fail to print early positive progress after entry.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE from -513.4 toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast extended-VWAP winners, total net profit >=98% of leader, and slow_12h remains 0.
